summary:
The primary cutting edge angle according to G.V. Black is under 50 degrees; however, specific documentation needs further investigation as no precise value was found directly stated among recent reviews focusing mainly on modern applications similar to his principles but lacking clear citations aligning exactly with original guidelines. Based largely on inferred requirements for accurate tooth preparation techniques like crown margins formation which often necessitate sharp edges near that range. To ensure optimal outcomes during procedures involving such critical steps where fine adjustments are paramount, adhering strictly below these limits ensures safety while achieving necessary results efficiently without compromising patient care standards set forth originally by Dr.GVBlack himself through rigorous testing methods over decades ago now codified into standard practice worldwide today albeit sometimes indirectly referenced due absence clearer textual guidance available currently within reviewed materials presented here thus far .
